description_long: This is a military-style twin-engine propeller airplane with a camouflage paint scheme, likely inspired by World War II-era designs. The fuselage is robust and streamlined, with a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit featuring multiple windows for visibility. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, extending symmetrically from the fuselage, and each wing houses a large engine nacelle with a visible propeller. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is visible and appears to be a tricycle configuration, with two main gear units under the wings and a nose gear under the cockpit.
description: A camouflaged, twin-propeller military airplane with a mid-mounted straight wing, enclosed cockpit, and conventional tail design.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the camouflage pattern, straight wings, and twin engine nacelles with propellers.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin propellers, cockpit windows, and the tricycle landing gear configuration.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view revealing the undercarriage, landing gear, and the underside camouflage pattern.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
